summary: A G7 economy, NATO founding member, and EU pillar with chronic political instability and high public debt, home to the world's largest cultural heritage sector and a powerful industrial north.

history.detail: Multiple kingdoms unified under the House of Savoy.
Promised territories; received less than expected (mutilated victory).
Fascist takeover; Mussolini becomes PM, then Duce (dictator).
Allied with Nazi Germany; disastrous military campaigns in Africa and Greece.
Allied invasion; Mussolini deposed, then reinstated by Germans in north.
Christian Democrats dominate Cold War politics.
Signs Treaty of Rome; European project co-founder.
Corruption investigations destroy traditional party system; Berlusconi era begins.

finance.currency_role: EUR; Italy is third-largest eurozone economy

resources.oil_gas: Minimal domestic; major importer; was dependent on Russia
resources.water: Po Valley water stress; Venice flooding
resources.agriculture: Olive oil, wine, tomatoes — major exporter

follow_the_money.detail: ~$50B/yr; largest cultural tourism sector in Europe
Estimated €100B underground economy linked to organised crime

theories_disputed.detail: CIA/NATO-backed stay-behind network in Italy; linked to political violence during 'Years of Lead'. Admitted by Italian PM Andreotti 1990.

connections.detail: NATO ally; US bases at Aviano, Sigonella
Major oil importer; Mattei formula energy agreements
